Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Case
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michelle D. Volk, esq. (619-234-2842) of J.W. Howard Attorneys for Plaintiff KC FUNDING LLC DBA KIDS CONNECTION via Court Call.
Yuanqiang Bai (650-208-4488) Defendant in propria persona.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KC FUNDING LLC DBA KIDS CONNECTION's Motion To Transfer Case.

Plaintiff KC Funding, LLC dba Kids Connection's motion to change venue is denied. The venue selection clause provides that the matter should be filed in the "Central District" of the Superior Court. Plaintiff fails to show that the "Central District" is San Mateo County. The fact that the contract specified San Mateo as the place of performance is distinct from the meaning of the venue selection clause. In addition, this "wrong court" motion is untimely and plaintiff waived the venue issue. Plaintiff chose San Francisco venue when it filed this action on March 29, 2018, and defendants did not move to change venue at that time. (See People v. Simon (2001) 25 Cal.4th 1082, 1102 ["any objection that a civil action has been brought in the wrong venue must be raised by a defendant specifically and at the very outset of the proceeding or it will be considered to have been forfeited."]). Plaintiff concedes that venue in San Francisco was proper at the time of filing. Plaintiff has made no evidentiary showing that a transfer to San Mateo County will promote the convenience of witnesses and/or interests of justice, and has improperly raised the issue for the first time on reply.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
